#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

### Post by JediSpam on 2006-12-22
right now my only options are 640x480 at 85 hz and 800x600 at 85 hz. my monitor is shaking of course. i want 1280 x 1024 at i guess 65 or 60 hz. whatever won't own my monitor

### Post by fragos on 2006-12-22
Your modline statements call out 60Hz.  Your xorg.conf looks a little confused to me.  I don't know what an A90f+ is.  If its LCD you should use 60Hz.  You may be trying many things without removing what didn't work.

---

### Post by JediSpam on 2006-12-23
ah it's a 19" crt. i'm not sure whats wrong and i can't figure it out

---

### Post by fragos on 2006-12-23
OK -- Viewsonic A90F+ 19" CRT with a maximum resoltion of 1,792 x 1,344 @ 61 Hz.  I believe there may be problems with your xorg.conf.  First let's create a new one by running the following on the command line.  If you're uncomfotable with an option use their default.

sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg

There are three factors that effect what resolutions will be used.  First is the monitor itself.  Second is what's in xorg and third is what that particular driver will support.  An open source driver choice can limit what resolutions are available to you regardless of what's in xorg.conf.  Lets go from here and see where we get.

---
